$comment: Canonical strings the skills must emit and the evaluators must match.
SKILL.md stays self-contained at runtime (npx skills add ships only SKILL.md),
so these strings live in the specs too — this file is the authority they are
checked against, mechanically, by scripts/check-format.py at release and in CI.
Edit a string here and in every file listed under `files`, or the check fails.
fenced_must_contain: the one block that actually gets copied. A rule can be word-perfect in the prose and absent from the template, and then nobody ever emits it.

required_verbatim is the only parity mechanism for the hand-maintained <plugin>/ copy, and it is deliberately narrow: it pins the literals an evaluator MATCHES. Everything else in that copy is unguarded — stated here so nobody mistakes green for parity.

forbidden_everywhere.$comment: A removed behavior leaves no notice behind: the spec reads as if it never existed. Scanned across markdown AND the shipped yaml/json entry shells — residue in an entry shell ships just the same.

review-loop.$comment_prefix: Evaluator ⑦ matches this literal. It MUST be a prefix of every marker form below — that is the check that catches a stray backtick or a renamed marker silently disarming the cap.
required_verbatim: A CR APPROVE without its deliverable is not a pass
review-loop.$comment_echo: The status block is what the evaluator reads. A renamed label is a predicate that silently stops firing.

openspec-apply-change-subagent.$comment_echo: The tier echo is keyed per expert: the 数据 group can dispatch two, and two identical markers hide a dropped one.

council.$comment_echo: The auditor parses the seat echo literally (A2). A drifted format is an audit that cannot reconcile.

catalog.$comment: Checked against a real checkout when one is present at ~/.agency-agents; skipped with a notice otherwise. This is what proves the path table is not fiction — the flaw that let `Application Security Engineer` resolve to nothing on a clean install.

families.$comment: Applied to every checked file, not per-skill: a family is only closed where nobody forgot it.

allowed: APPROVE-DEGRADED (bloat: +N over 2 rounds, no lane removed anything)
STOPPED (no real experts: a second axis has no match)
STOPPED (no real experts: none on the opposing axis)

families.$comment: Evaluator ⑫ matches these literally. A stray capital or backtick here disarms the check that a claimed strongest-tier expert was ever dispatched.
A pathless local marker is the cheapest lie the ladder names; it is not a legal form.
The prerequisite halt is a hand-back, not a verdict; one canonical form.
